2605.06595.title: Cross-Modal Navigation with Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
2605.06595.paper_summary: Robust embodied navigation relies on complementary sensory cues. However, high-quality and well-aligned multi-modal data is often difficult to obtain in practice. Training a monolithic model is also challenging as rich multi-modal inputs induce complex representations and substantially enlarge the policy space. Cross-modal collaboration among lightweight modality-specialized agents offers a scalable paradigm. It enables flexible deployment and parallel execution, while preserving the strength of each modality. In this paper, we propose \textbf{CRONA}, a Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MARL) framework for \textbf{Cro}ss-Modal \textbf{Na}vigation. CRONA improves collaboration by leveraging control-relevant auxiliary beliefs and a centralized multi-modal critic with global state. Experiments on visual-acoustic navigation tasks show that multi-agent methods significantly improve performance and efficiency over single-agent baselines. We find that homogeneous collaboration with limited modalities is sufficient for short-range navigation under salient cues; heterogeneous collaboration among agents with complementary modalities is generally efficient and effective; and navigation in large, complex environments requires both richer multi-modal perception and increased model capacity.
2605.06595.paper_summary_zh: 穩健的具身導航依賴於互補的感官線索。<lb/>然而，在實踐中，獲得高質量且良好對齊的多模態數據通常是困難的。<lb/>訓練一個單一的模型也很具挑戰性，因為豐富的多模態輸入會引發複雜的表示，並大幅擴大策略空間。<lb/>輕量級模態專門代理之間的跨模態協作提供了一種可擴展的範式。<lb/>它使靈活的部署和並行執行成為可能，同時保留每種模態的優勢。<lb/>在本文中，我們提出了\textbf{CRONA}，一個用於\textbf{跨}模態\textbf{導航}的多代理強化學習（MARL）框架。<lb/>CRONA通過利用與控制相關的輔助信念和具有全局狀態的集中式多模態評論者來改善協作。<lb/>在視覺-聲學導航任務上的實驗顯示，多代理方法在性能和效率上顯著優於單代理基準。<lb/>我們發現，對於在顯著線索下的短距離導航，同質的有限模態協作是足夠的；而具有互補模態的代理之間的異質協作通常是高效且有效的；在大型複雜環境中的導航則需要更豐富的多模態感知和增加的模型容量。
2605.06595.author: Shuo Liu et.al.
2605.06595.authors: Shuo Liu, Xinzichen Li, Christopher Amato
